Philosophy: Guilds in Light and Shadow are designed as lightweight social coordination structures, prioritizing group cohesion and individual ownership while strictly avoiding geopolitical territorial dominance or collective economic engines.

CanonicalRules.Text: Guilds function as small-scale social coordination groups and are not territorial, political, or economic control entities.
Guilds are limited to a maximum of 30 members.
Guilds use a fixed three-rank hierarchy: Leader, Officer, Member.
There are no formal alliance systems between guilds.
Guilds do not have shared banks or collective storage systems.
Guild membership does not prevent PvP interactions outside safe zones.
Guild creation has no currency cost and is freely available to eligible players.
Guild houses are acquired exclusively through gold payments.
Guild Houses function as social and utility hubs providing services such as crafting stations, NPC interactions, and quest/contract boards.
Guild systems are designed as lightweight social structures and must not extend into territorial control or global economic dominance.

SharedStorage.DesignIntent: Prevent collective economic monopolies and guild tax systems. Items remain individually owned. Trust is strictly social.

AllianceSystem.DesignIntent: Avoid massive zerg alliances and mega-coalitions. Small-scale group identity is preserved.

PvPBehavior.DesignIntent: Open world is inherently dangerous. Trust is a resource that must be actively managed by players. Betrayal is a mechanical possibility.

CreationFee.Description: Absolutely free to create. Encourages organic social grouping without artificial economic gates.

ProvidedUtility: Trophy rooms and visual guild identity banners
